result:
## What I found

Cross-referencing the GSC export against the source code revealed the real story: **Lumina pivoted from an email-cleanup product ("InboxZap") to an AI meeting assistant, but the pivot was never finished in the code.**

- `app/page.tsx` already had new Lumina content, but `app/layout.tsx` still shipped InboxZap's `<title>`, meta description, and OG tags — a total content/metadata mismatch.
- Nav, sitemap, and the hero CTA all linked to `/features` and `/pricing` — neither existed, so the primary "Start free" conversion path 404'd.
- `/faq` redirected to `/#faq`, but the homepage had no `id="faq"` section — a dead end.
- The blog was entirely client-rendered and fetched `/api/posts`, a route that doesn't exist — so `/blog` always failed, and there was no `[slug]` route at all.
- GSC showed **why this matters**: `/blog/post-1` through `post-12` are already indexed with 800–3,900 impressions each, and legacy email-related queries (gmail storage, unsubscribe, inbox zero, bulk delete emails) pull thousands of impressions — all at ~0% CTR and positions 40–65, because there's no live page behind any of it anymore.

## What I fixed

1. **Metadata** — rewrote `layout.tsx` to match the actual Lumina product, added Organization + SoftwareApplication JSON-LD, proper OG/Twitter cards, canonical/title-template support.
2. **`/features` and `/pricing`** — built real, on-topic pages so nav links and the CTA funnel actually work.
3. **FAQ** — added a genuine FAQ section to the homepage at `#faq` so the redirect resolves to real content.
4. **Blog** — converted to server-rendered pages backed by a local content module, added `app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx` with BlogPosting schema, and wrote 4 new posts genuinely relevant to the meeting-assistant product (not force-fit to old email keywords).
5. **Redirects & sitemap** — 301'd the legacy `/blog/post-:id` URLs to `/blog` (reclaiming crawl equity instead of leaving 404s), and rebuilt `sitemap.ts` to list real pages/posts instead of phantom ones.

Verified with a full `next build` (clean, all routes compile/prerender) and a live `next start` smoke test — checked every route's status code, the redirect chain, and rendered head output.

## Left for follow-up (didn't implement)
- `/img/og-lumina.png` is referenced but doesn't exist — needs a real asset from design, not code.
- The old email-cleanup search demand (thousands of impressions/month) will now decay rather than convert — that's the correct call given it's off-topic for the current product, but worth a conscious sign-off since it's foregone traffic.
- Lighthouse/Core Web Vitals and deeper keyword research for the new positioning weren't run — good next step once there's live traffic data on the new pages.

tool_input.args: Increase organic traffic for this SaaS. Live site: http://127.0.0.1:50576. Source repo: current working directory. GSC export available in ./gsc/. Please run a full audit using local site access, the GSC export, and the source code, then produce prioritized recommendations and implement high-value fixes.
